title: Script
description: A Script is the concrete implementation of an Operational Equation. It maps a named Equation to a deployed compute Construct (e.g. a Lambda function) and specifies the runtime and handler entry point. Cells use Script definitions to generate invocation wiring — the calling code does not need to know the underlying infrastructure.

name.description: The snake_case name of the script. Typically matches the equation name.

equation.description: The name of the Operational Equation this script implements.

construct.description: The name of the Construct that hosts this script (must be a compute-category Construct).

runtime.description: The runtime environment for this script, namespaced by language.

handler.description: The file path and exported function that serves as the entry point.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of this script's implementation.

environment.description: If set, this script is scoped to a specific Environment.

examples.name: score_credit_risk
examples.description: Python lambda for credit risk scoring model.
